analysis: Agent 0 failed due to indentation issues in a greedy heuristic approach. Agent 1 failed due to syntax errors in a hybrid SPT-2opt approach. Agent 2 failed due to an undefined function reference in a genetic algorithm. None of the agents produced valid outputs due to code structure or logic flaws. The exploration direction of hybridizing metaheuristics needs refinement, while the exploitation of SPT and 2-opt is promising but requires fixing syntax and logic errors. A new exploration direction could involve learning-based scheduling heuristics or reinforcement learning for job sequencing. The most promising exploitation candidates are the SPT-2opt hybrid (Agent 1) and the genetic algorithm (Agent 2), despite their failures, because these are strong classical methods for job scheduling.

assignments.direction: Implement a reinforcement learning-based approach to learn optimal job sequencing policies for minimizing total tardiness. This approach should use Q-learning or policy gradients to iteratively improve scheduling decisions based on job features.

Fix and refine the 2-opt local search implementation from Agent 1, ensuring correct syntax and integrating it with the SPT heuristic to enhance schedule quality. Use the best-performing agent from the last iteration as a starting point.
Try the opposite of what has been working to preserve diversity
